T0147.title: Card Deduction
T0147.definition: An unauthorized payment method that bypasses the need for a physical card, requiring only account details and a verification code.
T0147.description: Once a cardholder's number, name, ID, phone number, and SMS verification code are compromised, fraudsters can initiate a card-not-present transaction, circumventing the physical card and its PIN. This technique is common in the fund-draining phase following information theft via phishing sites or trojans. The transfer is swift, and victims often only realize something is wrong upon receiving a debit notification.
T0147.usageExample: He clicked a link and filled in his details, and then a card-not-present fraud happened. The money was drained in three transactions; he only knew when the bank's text alerts came through.
